metadata.purpose: Performance benchmark framework for tensor fusion, parallelism scaling, and compile-time overhead measurement

tool.name: Criterion
tool.description: Statistical benchmarking framework for Rust. Provides warmup, multiple iterations, outlier detection, and HTML reports.
tool.setup: Add criterion = { version = "0.5", features = ["html_reports"] } to [dev-dependencies]. Add [[bench]] entries to Cargo.toml.

benchmarkSuite.name: Tensor Fusion Throughput
benchmarkSuite.description: Compare fused vs unfused tensor operation throughput for each fusion pattern.

benchmark.name: linear_relu_fused_vs_unfused
benchmark.description: matmul(A, B) + bias + relu: fused single-kernel vs 3 separate ops

variants.variant: matmul → add → relu (3 kernel calls, 2 intermediate tensors)

benchmark.name: mse_fused_vs_unfused
benchmark.description: MSE loss: subtract + square + mean, fused vs unfused

benchmark.name: softmax_ce_fused_vs_unfused
benchmark.description: Softmax + cross-entropy: numerically stable fused vs naive unfused

benchmark.name: elementwise_chain_scaling
benchmark.description: Chain of N element-wise ops on same-shaped tensor. Measure speedup as N grows.

benchmark.name: broadcast_binary_fused_vs_unfused
benchmark.description: broadcast + binary op: fused avoids materializing broadcasted tensor

variants.variant: broadcast A to 1000x1000, then add B

benchmarkSuite.name: Parallel Scaling
benchmarkSuite.description: Measure parallel speedup across thread counts for independent tensor workloads.

benchmark.name: independent_matmuls_scaling
benchmark.description: N independent matrix multiplications dispatched to thread pool

benchmark.name: dag_width_vs_speedup
benchmark.description: Vary DAG width (number of independent ops) and measure scaling
benchmark.setup: Tensor ops of fixed size, varying independence count: 1, 2, 4, 8, 16
benchmark.expected: Speedup grows with width up to thread count, then plateaus

benchmark.name: thread_pool_overhead
benchmark.description: Measure dispatch/join overhead of thread pool vs direct sequential execution
benchmark.setup: Single trivial operation (1x1 matmul) dispatched vs called directly

benchmark.name: mixed_parallel_sequential
benchmark.description: Realistic workload with mix of parallelizable and sequential ops
benchmark.setup: Neural net forward pass: 2 independent branches merge at concat layer
benchmark.expected: ~2x speedup on 4 cores for dual-branch architecture

benchmarkSuite.name: Compile Time Overhead
benchmarkSuite.description: Measure compile-time overhead introduced by new MIR passes. Target: < 15% total overhead.

benchmark.name: baseline_no_passes
benchmark.description: Compile standard benchmark program with no optimization passes enabled

programs.program: Large: 1000 lines, 50 functions, complex tensor pipeline

benchmark.name: per_pass_overhead
benchmark.passes: dead_code, const_fold, linearize, float_combinators, fuse_ops, inline, parallelize

benchmark.name: all_passes_combined
benchmark.description: Compile with all passes enabled — measure total overhead

benchmarkSuite.name: Memory Usage
benchmarkSuite.description: Track peak memory usage for compilation and for runtime tensor operations.

benchmark.name: compiler_memory
benchmark.description: Peak RSS during compilation with all passes enabled

benchmark.name: fusion_memory_savings
benchmark.description: Runtime peak memory with fused vs unfused: measure intermediate tensor elimination
benchmark.programs: Element-wise chain of 10 ops on 1M element tensor

benchmark.name: interner_memory
benchmark.description: NameInterner memory usage vs baseline String identifiers
benchmark.expected: Interner uses less memory due to deduplication

numericalEquivalence.description: All fusion patterns must produce results within epsilon of unfused computation. This is a correctness requirement, not a performance benchmark.

regressionDetection.policy: Any benchmark regressing > 10% from golden baseline triggers CI warning.
